insight: When Elo ratings differ by less than 100 points, the underdog wins 34% of the time — nearly 1 in 3.
Grass produces the most upsets of any surface — favourites lose 32% of the time, vs 27% on hard courts.
Even with a 100-150 Elo point advantage, favourites still lose 28.6% of matches.
Heavily fatigued favourites (4+ recent matches) win 84% — fatigue rarely hurts the better player.
Big servers hitting 16+ aces win only 52.8% — high ace counts correlate with more double faults and risk.
Players committing 7+ double faults win just 54.1% of matches — each double fault is a gift to the opponent.
A 100-150 Elo point gap translates to a 71.4% win rate for the favourite — a reliable edge.
When Elo ratings differ by 500+ points, favourites win 96% of the time — near-certain outcomes.
When Elo ratings are nearly equal (0-50 gap), the higher-ranked player still wins 59% of the time.
Players winning 75-80% of first serve points have a 56.1% match win rate — serve dominance matters.
With a 300+ Elo point advantage, upsets occur just 13.8% of the time — the model is most reliable here.
Indoor hard courts produce more upsets (29.1%) than outdoor hard courts (26.9%) — conditions matter.
